problem.title: Every teacher knows this feeling
problem.description: You look at your class and you know something isn't working for some students, but when you detect it, it's already too late to act in time.

items.title: Scattered observations
items.description: Valuable observations end up in isolated notes, hallway conversations, or fuzzy memories.

items.title: Late interventions
items.description: When a student falls behind, you often don't notice in time to help.

items.title: Blind spots
items.description: Without a systematic record, important patterns in each student's progress are lost.

items.title: Mental load
items.description: You try to remember everything while teaching, but memory has limits.

closing.rest: Mehmiro exists so you never lose an important observation again.

solution.title: Meet Mehmiro
solution.description: The lens that accompanies teaching. Technology as listening for teachers, not control.

features.title: Your judgment is the foundation
features.description: Mehmiro doesn't evaluate: it interprets. It amplifies your pedagogical lens without replacing it.

features.title: AI as a tool
features.description: An intelligent agent that organizes and analyzes your observations to reveal hidden patterns.

features.title: Early detection
features.description: Identify students who need attention before it's too late.

features.title: Teacher metacognition
features.description: Reflect on your own practice with reports generated from your records.

features.title: Professionalize observation
features.description: Turn valuable intuitions into systematic, actionable evidence.

features.title: No added load
features.description: It integrates naturally into your workflow. Less paperwork, more teaching.

solution.valueProposition: It doesn't evaluate: it interprets. Your judgment is the base.

socialProof.title: Join schools already using it
socialProof.description: Teachers and schools in Argentina are already transforming their pedagogical observation with Mehmiro.

testimonials.quote: Mehmiro helps me see patterns I used to miss. It's like having an assistant who never forgets anything I observe.
testimonials.author: María González

testimonials.quote: For the first time I have systematic evidence of each student's progress. Family meetings are now more productive.
testimonials.author: Carlos Rodríguez

testimonials.quote: It doesn't add workload, it integrates naturally into my practice. The reports help me reflect on my own teaching.

faq.title: Answers to your questions
faq.description: We address the main concerns of teachers and institutions.

items.question: How long does it take to implement Mehmiro?
items.answer: You can start seeing results within the first 24 hours. The platform integrates naturally into your current workflow without adding significant administrative load.

items.question: Does it replace a teacher's professional judgment?
items.answer: Quite the opposite. Mehmiro is designed to strengthen your pedagogical lens. You remain at the center of the evaluation. Technology is a tool that amplifies your observation capacity, not replaces it.

items.question: What happens to student data?
items.answer: Privacy and data security are our priority. We comply with all data protection regulations in Argentina. Data belongs to the institution and is stored securely.

items.question: What types of institutions is Mehmiro for?
items.answer: Mehmiro is designed for schools at all educational levels, from primary to secondary and teacher training. It adapts to different contexts and pedagogical needs.

items.question: Do I need training to use it?
items.answer: No. Mehmiro is designed to be intuitive and easy to use. You can start recording observations from day one without prior training.

items.question: Does Mehmiro monitor or evaluate teachers?
items.answer: No. Mehmiro is a pedagogical support tool that respects your professional autonomy. It doesn't judge your work; it strengthens your judgment. The AI analyzes your observations to help you see patterns, not to evaluate you.

cta.title: Ready to transform your teaching practice?
cta.description: Join over 500 teachers already using Mehmiro to support their students' learning.

objectives: Understand how to claim your progress

steps.title: Access without signing up
steps.description: Mehmiro lets you try the app without creating an account. Click 'Try the free demo' and you get an anonymous 7-day trial. You can read and write freely.
steps.tip: If you already have a Google account, you can sign in directly and skip the trial.

steps.title: AI onboarding
steps.description: The AI guides you to set up your first class. Describe your subject, grade and school in natural language. The AI generates a draft student list, assessment variables and pedagogical preferences.
steps.tip: Be as specific as possible: the AI uses context to personalize the entire flow.

steps.title: Explore the dashboard
steps.description: The dashboard groups your classes by institution, shows configuration status and redirects you to the daily schedule as the main entry point.
steps.tip: If you belong to multiple institutions, you'll see each one separately with its classes.

steps.title: Claim your progress
steps.description: When the 7-day trial expires, you can still read evaluations but not write. If you register with an account, you recover everything you did during the trial.
steps.tip: Register before the trial expires so you don't lose anything.

steps.title: Create your institution
steps.description: Classes are no longer created with free-text school names. They're now linked to a real institution with memberships. Each institution has its own space with logo, pedagogical preferences and allowed email list.
steps.tip: If you've already been invited to an institution, you don't need to create a new one.

steps.title: Roles and permissions
steps.description: There are four roles: principal/owner (full control), admin/administrator (class and teacher management), teacher (class teacher) and assistant (helper). Each role has different permissions.
steps.tip: The principal role can see all classes in the institution, even without being a direct teacher.

steps.title: Invite teachers by email
steps.description: From the school info, manage a list of allowed emails. A principal can create a class and assign it to a teacher's email. If the teacher already accepted the invitation, the membership is created; if not, it stays pending.
steps.tip: The teacher must sign in with the same email they were invited with.

steps.title: School info
steps.description: Administrative roles edit pedagogical preferences, upload the logo and manage allowed emails. These preferences are used as context for AI suggestions across the institution.
steps.tip: Setting up preferences well improves the quality of AI suggestions.

steps.title: Add a student manually
steps.description: From the Student List, click 'Add student'. Fill in name, avatar color and personality type. Each student has a visual avatar with customizable color.
steps.tip: Personality type helps the AI better contextualize observations.

steps.title: Paste student list
steps.description: If you have a list of names in a document, paste it into the textarea. Mehmiro parses each line, previews the rows and detects duplicates before confirming bulk creation.
steps.tip: One name per line. If a student has first and last name, write them on the same line.

steps.title: Upload CSV
steps.description: Upload a CSV file with columns: name, dob, personalityType, avatarColor. Mehmiro shows a preview with validation before confirming bulk creation.
steps.tip: The 'name' column is required. The others are optional.

steps.title: Edit and archive
steps.description: You can edit the name directly from the list (inline editing). From the student profile, you can archive students: it hides them from the list but preserves all evaluation history.
steps.tip: Archiving doesn't delete data. History is preserved forever.

steps.title: Daily calendar view
steps.description: The schedule shows class sessions for the selected day. Each event can have an associated lesson/content. Click on an event to see the summary with session data and the linked lesson.
steps.tip: The schedule is the default entry point from the dashboard and onboarding.

steps.title: Shared date selector
steps.description: The date navigation bar is reused across roster, content, observations and student profiles. The date lives in the URL (date=YYYY-MM-DD), so you can share direct links.
steps.tip: Change the date from any page and it syncs across the entire class.

steps.title: Create session manually
steps.description: From the schedule, click to create a session. Fill in date, time and class data. From the same dialog you can create a new lesson in the same flow.
steps.tip: Creating a lesson alongside a session saves time.

steps.title: Deep-dive into sessions
steps.description: Clicking a schedule event opens a dialog with session data and the linked lesson, with a deep-link directly to the lesson card.
steps.tip: Deep-links let you share specific sessions with colleagues.

objectives: Create lessons manually and with AI
Navigate between Day view and Bank view

steps.title: Lesson list
steps.description: Lessons are displayed as a swipable card interface. There are two views: Day (content planned for the selected date) and Bank (orphan/candidate lessons not linked to any class session).
steps.tip: The Bank is useful for creating content in advance and scheduling it later.

steps.title: Create a lesson manually
steps.description: Fill out a form with title, date, description, objectives and activities. The lesson becomes available in the bank or linked to a session if you chose a date with an event.
steps.tip: Well-defined objectives help the AI generate better rubrics.

steps.title: Create a lesson with AI
steps.description: The AI generates two options using the selected date, class context, yearly plan and institutional preferences. The teacher chooses one, reviews the form and saves.
steps.tip: The AI becomes more accurate the more preferences the institution has configured.

steps.title: Schedule from the bank
steps.description: An orphan lesson can be linked directly to a class session from the schedule or from the bank. You can also edit and delete lessons from the active card.
steps.tip: Scheduling in advance lets you have everything ready before class.

objectives: Understand how the plan affects AI suggestions

steps.title: Access the yearly plan
steps.description: The yearly plan lives at /class/:id/yearly-plan. It's the space to load the yearly curriculum plan for the class, with title, content, teacher notes, source type and status.
steps.tip: The yearly plan is optional but greatly improves AI suggestions.

steps.title: AI draft
steps.description: The AI can generate a yearly plan from existing content and teacher adjustment notes. The draft is read-only until the teacher accepts and saves it.
steps.tip: Provide adjustment notes so the AI personalizes the plan to your context.

steps.title: Context for lessons
steps.description: AI suggestions for lessons now include the saved yearly plan as prompt context. This means suggested lessons are coherent with your planning.
steps.tip: A detailed yearly plan produces more accurate and relevant lessons.

steps.title: Edit and update
steps.description: The yearly plan isn't static. You can update it at any time as the school year progresses. Changes affect future AI suggestions.
steps.tip: Review the plan periodically to keep it current.

steps.title: Data model
steps.description: The rubric system includes: assessment_lenses (reusable archetypes), assessment_dimensions (student development dimensions), assessment_dimension_sets (dimension groups), assessment_scales (scoring scales), rubrics, rubric_criteria, rubric_level_descriptors and rubric_assessment_results.
steps.tip: You don't need to understand the entire model to get started. The AI helps you create rubrics automatically.

steps.title: Dimension separation
steps.description: There are two types of dimensions: institutional/global (coexistence, behavior, autonomy, collaboration) and subject-specific (technical knowledge, methodological, procedural).
steps.tip: Institutional dimensions are shared across all classes in the institution.

steps.title: Rubrics per lesson
steps.description: Each lesson can have its own rubric combining institutional dimensions with activity-specific criteria. The /class/:id/rubrics workspace replaces the legacy Variables surface.
steps.tip: Creating a rubric per lesson enables more specific and relevant evaluations.

steps.title: Reusable assessment lenses
steps.description: The AI can reason about evidence using archetypes like transfer, procedural fluency, communication, collaboration, self-regulation and creativity. Charts consume normalized values preserving criterion, scale, descriptor, source teacher, class, lesson, session and textual evidence.
steps.tip: Lenses let you evaluate the same aspect in different contexts.

steps.title: Stage 1: Free narrative
steps.description: Write what happened in class in natural language. No format required. Write as if you were telling a colleague what you observed. You can be as detailed or concise as you want.
steps.tip: Including student names and specific behavior descriptions helps the AI.

steps.title: Stage 2: Extraction review
steps.description: The AI identifies which students were mentioned (matching against the roster), extracts an observation fragment per student, and marks if it's analyzable for evaluation. You can edit fragments, remove misidentified students or resolve ambiguities.
steps.tip: The AI understands informal references, nicknames and initials. If there's ambiguity, it asks you to resolve.

steps.title: Stage 3: Per-student analysis
steps.description: For each approved pair, the AI suggests evaluation values according to the lesson/session rubric. The teacher applies or rejects per student. Nothing is saved automatically until you explicitly apply it.
steps.tip: You can reject suggestions that don't reflect your judgment. The AI is a tool, not a judge.

steps.title: Error handling and recovery
steps.description: If there's a quota limit (429) or AI error, the teacher is informed, remaining calls are stopped and state is saved for retry. The narrative, extracted rows and partial results are saved in session storage so you don't lose work if the modal closes.
steps.tip: If something fails, you can close and reopen without losing what you've already done.

steps.title: Profile page
steps.description: Each student has a page at /student/:id with their complete profile. Includes personal data, avatar, evaluation history and interactive charts.
steps.tip: Access the profile from the student list or from any student link.

steps.title: Interactive charts
steps.description: Charts include polar, radar and line with Chart.js showing evaluation history. You can filter by date, lesson, dimension and source teacher.
steps.tip: The radar chart is ideal for seeing a student's strengths and weaknesses at a glance.

steps.title: AI analysis
steps.description: The AI analysis button generates an individual report for the student based on their evaluation history and the teacher's observation. The flow includes approval and export.
steps.tip: AI analysis is a starting point, not a diagnosis. Your judgment always prevails.

steps.title: Insight history
steps.description: Reports written by the teacher have an approval/export flow. You can generate reports to share with families, counselors or other teachers.
steps.tip: Approved insights can be exported as PDF for family meetings.

forEducators.tipsDescription: Practical tips from teachers who already use Mehmiro effectively

tips.title: Record in the moment
tips.description: Observations are more accurate when you record them immediately after they occur. Use the voice notes feature if you can't write at the moment.

tips.title: Be specific but concise
tips.description: Avoid generalizations like 'good' or 'bad'. Describe exactly what the student did or said. 'Completed 3 of 5 exercises' is more useful than 'worked well'.

tips.title: Use the categories
tips.description: Mehmiro's categories are designed to cover the most important aspects of development. Try to use at least 2-3 categories per observation.

tips.title: Review patterns
tips.description: Don't just look at individual observations. Weekly, review the patterns that Mehmiro detects. They are invaluable for understanding real progress.

tips.title: Act in time
tips.description: Early detection alerts are tools, not diagnoses. Use them as a starting point to converse, intervene and support.

tips.title: Share with the team
tips.description: Collaboration multiplies the impact. Share relevant observations with tutors, counselors and other teachers working with the same student.

support.title: Support
support.description: Access technical help, quick guides and resources to resolve platform questions.
support.heroDescription: Have questions? Find answers to the most common questions or contact our support team.

questions.q: How do I create an account on Mehmiro?
questions.a: Simply click 'Start for free' on our main page and follow the steps. You only need an email and your institution's details.

questions.q: What if I forgot my password?
questions.a: Click 'Forgot my password' on the login page. We'll send you an email to reset your password.

questions.q: Can I use Mehmiro on multiple devices?
questions.a: Yes, Mehmiro is a progressive web app. You can access it from your computer, tablet or phone, and your data will sync automatically.

questions.q: How long does it take to record an observation?
questions.a: Recording a basic observation takes between 2-3 minutes. You can use voice notes that automatically convert to text, which speeds up the process.
These are notifications the system generates when it detects patterns that could indicate a student needs more attention. These alerts are based on your observations and help you intervene in time.

questions.q: How do I customize observation categories?
questions.a: You can access Settings > Categories from your dashboard. There you can create, edit or delete categories according to your institution's needs.
Yes, you can export your data in PDF or Excel format from the Reports section. You can also generate specific reports to share with families.
Absolutely. We use end-to-end encryption and comply with the Argentine Personal Data Protection Law. The data belongs to your institution.

questions.q: Who can see the observations I record?
questions.a: By default, only you and users from your institution with assigned permissions. You can configure granular permissions from the admin panel.

questions.q: What if I want to delete my account?
questions.a: You can request deletion of your account and all associated data at any time. Contact us at support@mehmiro.com.
We have a free plan with basic features and paid plans for institutions that need more capacity. Visit our pricing page for more details.

questions.q: Is there a discount for public educational institutions?
questions.a: Yes, we offer special terms for public schools and non-profits. Contact us to discuss the options.

questions.q: Can I change plans at any time?
questions.a: Yes, you can upgrade or downgrade your plan at any time. Changes will apply on the next billing cycle.

about.title: About us
about.description: Meet the team behind Mehmiro and our mission to support formative assessment in schools.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: Democratize access to quality formative assessment tools for all schools in Argentina and Latin America. We want every teacher to have the ability to detect in time when a student needs more support, without adding administrative burden to their work.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.description: Imagine a future where technology enhances teaching, where every educator can trust their intuition backed by evidence. An educational ecosystem where no student falls behind due to lack of early detection.

values.title: Respect for Teachers
values.description: The teacher is the protagonist. Our technology is designed to amplify their gaze, never to replace it. Their professional judgment is the center of everything we do.

values.title: Human-Centered Approach
values.description: Behind every data point is a student with potential. We design thinking about people, not numbers. Each feature responds to a real classroom need.

values.title: Privacy and Security
values.description: Your students' data is sacred. We comply with all data protection regulations in Argentina and prioritize security above all.

story.title: Our Story
story.author: Mehmiro team
paragraphs: Mehmiro was born after years of living through the tensions of the educational system from multiple roles: first as a student, then as a teacher, later as a trainer, and also participating in teams designing and implementing innovative educational public policies.
In all these spaces, a recurring problem: academic evidence about how people learn rarely manages to translate into simple and usable tools in the real classroom.

The startup emerges as a bridge between learning science research and the daily practice of teachers. Mehmiro seeks to turn theory into concrete action, without imposing external methodologies or increasing workload, but rather enhancing professional judgment with technology that organizes, makes visible, and scales what good teachers already do intuitively.

cta.title: Want to be part of our story?
cta.description: Join the teachers who are already transforming their pedagogical practice with Mehmiro.

team.title: Team
team.description: Introducing the interdisciplinary team: research, design and educational development.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: Information about how we collect, use and protect institution and student data.

highlights.title: Your data is yours
highlights.description: We don't sell your information. The data belongs to your institution.

highlights.title: End-to-end encryption
highlights.description: Your observations and analysis are protected with the highest standards.

highlights.title: Full control
highlights.description: You have access, rectification and deletion of your data whenever you want.

highlights.title: Legal compliance
highlights.description: We comply with the Argentine Personal Data Protection Law (Law 25.326).

sections.title: 1. Introduction
sections.content:
At Mehmiro ("we", "our platform"), we are committed to protecting the privacy and security of our users' data, especially data of minor students which is treated with maximum care and protection.

This Privacy Policy describes how we collect, use, store and protect your personal information and that of the students at your institution. By using Mehmiro, you accept the practices described in this policy.

sections.title: 2. Information we collect
sections.content:
We collect different types of information to provide and improve our services:

• **Registration information**: When you create an account, we collect your name, email, educational institution and role (teacher, coordinator, director, etc.).

• **Student data**: To use the platform, you can register information about students including: name (or anonymous identifier), age, grade, pedagogical observations, progress evaluations and development notes.

• **Pedagogical observations**: All notes, voice recordings converted to text, photos, videos and analysis you make about students.

• **Usage data**: Information about how you interact with the platform, including usage metrics, preferences and settings.

• **Device information**: Technical data about the device you use to access Mehmiro.

sections.title: 3. How we use your information
sections.content:
We use the collected information for the following purposes:

• **Provide the service**: Manage your account, allow observation recording and generate analysis and reports.

• **AI analysis**: Our artificial intelligence system analyzes your observations to identify patterns, detect students who may need attention and offer intervention suggestions.

• **Personalization**: Adapt the user experience according to your preferences and needs.

• **Communication**: Send you notifications about early detection alerts, platform updates and technical support.

• **Service improvement**: Analyze aggregated and anonymized data to improve Mehmiro's functionalities.

• **Legal compliance**: Respond to legal requests and protect our rights and those of our users.

sections.title: 4. Data storage and security
sections.content:
The security of your data is our absolute priority:

• **Encryption**: All data is encrypted in transit (TLS/SSL) and at rest (AES-256) to guarantee that only you and your institution can access it.

• **Secure servers**: Our servers are located in Argentina and comply with the best physical and digital security practices.

• **Access control**: We implement strict access controls. Only authorized personnel can access the data, and always under security protocols.

• **Backup**: We perform regular automatic backups to guarantee the availability of your data.

• **Retention**: We keep data while your account is active or as necessary to provide services. You can request data deletion at any time.

sections.title: 5. Data sharing
sections.content:
Your data is yours. We don't sell it under any circumstances:

• **Institution**: Your institution's data is available to other authorized users of the same institution according to the permissions you set.

• **Service providers**: We share data with providers who help us operate the platform (hosting, analysis, technical support), who are obligated to maintain confidentiality.

• **Legal**: We may disclose information when required by law or in response to valid legal requests.

• **Transfers**: In the event of a merger or sale of the company, data could be transferred, but we will notify you and request your consent if required.

sections.title: 6. User rights
sections.content:
You have the following rights over your data:

• **Access**: You can request a copy of all data we have about you and your institution's students.

• **Rectification**: You can correct inaccurate or incomplete information.

• **Deletion**: You can request deletion of your data and student data (except when law requires retention).

• **Portability**: You can export your data in a machine-readable format.

• **Withdrawal of consent**: You can withdraw your consent for data processing at any time.

• **Limitation**: You can request that we limit the processing of certain data.

To exercise these rights, contact us through the channels indicated at the end of this policy.

sections.title: 7. Minor data
sections.content:
Mehmiro is designed for educational use and frequently involves data of minor students:

• **Parental consent**: We recommend that institutions obtain appropriate consent from parents or guardians before registering student data.

• **Special protection**: We apply additional protection measures to minor data.

• **Limitations**: We do not intentionally collect minor information beyond what's necessary for the agreed educational purpose.

• **Guardian rights**: Parents or legal guardians can exercise access, rectification and deletion rights on behalf of their minor children.

sections.title: 8. Cookies and similar technologies
sections.content:
We use cookies and similar technologies for:

• **Essential operation**: Keep your session active and remember your preferences.

• **Analytics**: Understand how the platform is used to improve it.

• **Security**: Detect and prevent fraudulent activities.

You can configure your browser to reject cookies, but this may affect platform functionality.

sections.title: 9. Changes to this policy
sections.content:
We may occasionally update this Privacy Policy. We will notify you of any significant changes through:

• Platform notifications
• Email to registered users
• Publishing the new version on our site

The last update date is indicated at the end of this document. We recommend reviewing this policy periodically.

sections.title: 10. Contact
sections.content:
If you have questions, concerns or requests related to this Privacy Policy or the processing of your data, you can contact us:

• **Email**: privacidad@mehmiro.com
• **Address**: Buenos Aires, Argentina

We will respond to your inquiry within 30 business days.

compliance.title: Regulatory Compliance
compliance.description: Mehmiro complies with the Argentine Personal Data Protection Law (Law 25.326) and its amendments, as well as with international best practices for privacy and minor data protection.

terms.title: Terms & Conditions
terms.description: Legal terms for using the Mehmiro platform for institutions and teachers.

sections.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
sections.content:
By accessing and using Mehmiro ("the Platform"), you ("User", "Institution" or "Teacher") agree to comply with these Terms and Conditions ("Terms").

If you do not agree to these Terms, you should not access or use the Platform.

Mehmiro reserves the right to modify these Terms at any time. Modifications will take effect upon publication on the Platform. Continued use of Mehmiro after modifications constitutes acceptance of the new Terms.

sections.title: 2. Service Description
sections.content:
Mehmiro is a digital formative assessment platform designed to support teaching work. The service includes:

• Registration and organization of pedagogical observations
• Pattern analysis through artificial intelligence
• Early detection of students requiring attention
• Report and documentation generation
• Collaboration between teachers and institutions

Mehmiro is a pedagogical support tool. It does not replace the teacher's professional judgment or constitute formal institutional assessment.

sections.title: 3. Eligibility and Registration
sections.content:
To use Mehmiro, the User must:

• Be over 18 years old or have legal guardian authorization
• Be a teacher, coordinator, director or other educational staff
• Provide truthful and updated information during registration
• Maintain confidentiality of their access credentials

Educational institutions can create institutional accounts. The authorized representative of the institution declares they have authority to bind the institution to these Terms.

sections.title: 4. User Accounts and Security
sections.content:
The User is responsible for:

• Maintaining confidentiality of their password and access data
• All activities occurring under their account
• Immediately notifying any unauthorized use
• Providing accurate and updated information

Mehmiro will not be liable for losses or damages arising from failure to comply with these obligations.

The Platform may use two-factor authentication for added security.

sections.title: 5. Student Data
sections.content:
The User acknowledges and agrees that:

• They are responsible for obtaining necessary consents from parents/guardians for processing minor student data
• They must comply with Law 25.326 on Personal Data Protection and its amendments
• Student data is the property of the educational institution
• They must use data only for legitimate educational purposes

Mehmiro acts as a data processor on behalf of institutions. Student data will not be used for AI model training beyond what is necessary to provide the service.

sections.title: 6. Acceptable Use
sections.content:
The User agrees NOT to use Mehmiro for:

• Illegal activities or those that infringe third-party rights
• Registering false, defamatory or violent information
• Sharing adult, offensive or discriminatory content
• Attempting to access other users' accounts
• Interfering with the Platform's operation
• Using the Platform in a way that may damage, disable or overload servers

Mehmiro reserves the right to suspend or cancel accounts that violate these terms.

sections.title: 7. Intellectual Property
sections.content:
Mehmiro and all its content, including but not limited to:

• Source code, algorithms and technology
• Designs, graphics and interfaces
• Trademarks and logos
• Documentation and marketing materials

Are property of Mehmiro and are protected by Argentine and international intellectual property laws.

The User retains ownership of the observations and content they register, but grants Mehmiro a limited license to operate the Platform and improve the Service.

sections.title: 8. Privacy and Data Protection
sections.content:
Personal data processing in Mehmiro is governed by our Privacy Policy, which is an integral part of these Terms.

By using Mehmiro, the User consents to the processing of their personal data and that of students as described in the Privacy Policy.

Mehmiro implements appropriate security measures to protect data, but cannot guarantee absolute security. The User must take their own precautions.

sections.title: 9. Plans and Payments
sections.content:
Mehmiro offers free and paid plans:

• **Free Plan**: Basic functionalities with user and storage limitations.
• **Paid Plans**: Full access according to the selected plan.

Paid plan prices are indicated on the Platform and may change with prior notice. Changes do not affect already paid subscriptions until renewal.

Payment is made per period (monthly or annual). Subscription renews automatically unless cancelled.

The User can cancel their subscription at any time from their account panel.

sections.title: 10. Limitation of Liability
sections.content:
Mehmiro is provided "as is" and "as available".

Mehmiro does not guarantee that:
• The service will be uninterrupted, secure or error-free
• The results obtained will be accurate or reliable
• The quality will meet User expectations

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Mehmiro will not be liable for:
• Indirect, incidental or consequential damages
• Loss of data, opportunities or income
• Costs of acquiring substitute goods

Mehmiro's total liability will not exceed the amount paid by the User in the last 12 months.

sections.title: 11. Indemnification
sections.content:
The User agrees to indemnify and hold harmless Mehmiro, its directors, employees and agents from any claim, demand, loss or expense (including legal fees) arising from:

• The User's use of the Platform
• Violation of these Terms
• Content the User registers on the Platform
• Student data under their responsibility

sections.title: 12. Termination
sections.content:
Mehmiro may terminate these Terms and cancel User access:

• For convenience, with prior notice
• Immediately for serious violation of the Terms
• By legal requirement

Upon termination:
• Platform access will be deactivated
• Data may be deleted according to the Retention Policy
• Outstanding obligations (payments) will remain in force

The User can request deletion of their account and data at any time.

sections.title: 13. Applicable Law and Dispute Resolution
sections.content:
These Terms are governed by the laws of the Argentine Republic.

Any dispute arising from these Terms will be resolved exclusively by the ordinary courts of the City of Buenos Aires, waiving any other jurisdiction that may correspond.

Before initiating litigation, the parties will attempt to resolve the conflict in good faith through direct negotiation.

sections.title: 14. General Provisions
sections.content:
• **Agreement integrity**: These Terms, together with the Privacy Policy, constitute the complete agreement between the parties.

• **Severability**: If any provision is declared invalid, the others will remain in force.

• **Waiver**: Mehmiro's failure to exercise a right does not constitute waiver to exercise it in the future.

• **Assignment**: The User cannot assign their rights under these Terms without Mehmiro's written consent.

• **Notifications**: Notifications will be considered valid when sent to the registered email or published on the Platform.

sections.title: 15. Contact
sections.content:
For any inquiries about these Terms:

• **Email**: legal@mehmiro.com
• **Address**: Buenos Aires, Argentina
• **Contact form**: mehmiro.com/contacto

We will respond to your inquiry within 10 business days.

steps.title: Juan, a teacher like you
steps.description: Same subject but different grades across two schools. Same content, different contexts. A colleague introduced him to Mehmiro and he decided to try it.

steps.title: Beatriz and Juan meet in the school hallway
steps.description: Juan shows her how Mehmiro analyzes what he observes about each student while they work. This allows Mehmiro to offer support and scaffolding for each difficulty each student faces. Beatriz wants to know more and Juan opens a student's profile.

steps.title: Beatriz discovers the potential
steps.description: She realizes that Mehmiro doesn't just help Juan, it can also transform her own way of teaching and connecting with her students.

step1.intro: Tell us about you, your school, and your students. Your name, subject, institution, how many students you have, and the grade.
step1.submit: When you submit, your profile and your class are created with your students. Keep scrolling to see the process video.

step2.variables: Assessment variables, aspects that Mehmiro extracts from your observations. You can edit them if you want.
step2.observation: Here you record your observation about the student's work.
step2.analysis: Here Mehmiro analyzes the student's history and will provide a report on their overall status with support to improve weaker areas of learning.
